summary: Opus 3-tier catalyst sweep (chunk 15 (PLUG-RLJ)): scan -> deep -> skeptic

survivors_flagged.one_line: The Doctors Company acquiring ProAssurance $25.00 cash (stk $24.71, ~1.2% spread); shareholders+FTC+7 states cleared, only CA regulator pending, close by Jun 30 2026 — clean dated arb.

LIVE controlling-shareholder (Priore, 58%) take-private at $6.00-6.15, special committee evaluating, Buckley+Steamboat publicly oppose as lowball ($15-20 fair value); trades $5.82 BELOW bid floor = mispriced squeeze-out asymmetry.

Rusfertide (first-in-class hepcidin mimetic for PV) NDA on Priority Review with hard Q3-2026 PDUFA; Takeda opt-out already paid $200m and approval triggers +$200m/+$75m plus 14-29% royalties; small-cap with real asymmetry and buyout chatter.

Palvella's QTORIN rapamycin hit Ph3 SELVA primary endpoint; Pre-NDA meeting completed Jun-3-2026 with NDA filing on track for H2-2026 in microcystic lymphatic malformations (NO approved therapy) — forward regulatory path with first-to-market asymmetry.

Live take-private: Irth Capital + largest US franchisee (~10%) bid ~$47/sh; stock $31.92 = ~47% gross spread still open; June 1 2026 board fiduciary-duty investigation signals process underway. Unfired, wide arb asymmetry.
Skyworks merger ($32.50 cash + 0.960 SWKS/sh, ~$22B) shareholder-approved; FTC Second Requests create antitrust overhang = potential mispriced asymmetry. Closes early 2027 — live cash-and-stock arb.

Jacobs building-products roll-up: Kodiak ($2.25B) acquisition closing early Q2 2026 (imminent) plus serial-M&A optionality. Dated, but mostly the company's own program rather than an arb in the stock.
Definitive all-cash merger: Publicis at $38.50/sh ($2.5B), both boards approved; stock $37.42 = ~2.9% spread. Pending vote + antitrust/CFIUS, must close by May 2027. Clean dated arb.
UX111 (Sanfilippo A) BLA on Priority Review with PDUFA action date Sept 19 2026 — hard, dated accelerated-approval binary; 8-yr durability data supports asymmetry. Unfired.
Casdatifan ccRCC: mid-2026 ARC-20 IO-experienced readout + ongoing PEAK-1 Ph3 and new BMS combo collab; one real dated clinical readout, pipeline-binary not classic special-sit.

Third FDA bite at RP1 melanoma BLA: twice-rejected, Apr-2026 PDUFA passed w/o approval, but May 29 2026 FDA aligned on fresh resubmission after White House intervention + Makary exit — forward dated binary, mispriced asymmetry, stock ~$9.47 off highs (new PDUFA TBD).

ADI Global Distribution spin-off live: Form 10-12B/A on file, distribution of 100% of ADI to REZI holders expected to complete 2H 2026 (record date TBD). Forward, not fired; own RemainCo into separation. Two Bloomberg-dated gates (registration effective + distribution).

Transocean + Valaris all-stock combination (PREM14A filed 2026): Bermuda scheme of arrangement, ~53/47 split, support agreements from Perestroika (9%) and Oak Hill/Famatown (18%) locked. Dated close expected 2H 2026 pending shareholder votes + regulatory. Complicated cross-border merger-arb; some offshore-cycle macro overlay.

verified.catalyst: The Doctors Company is acquiring ProAssurance for $25.00/share all-cash; only the California insurance regulator's approval remains before closing.
verified.dated_milestone: Anticipated deal close by June 30, 2026 (per June 2, 2026 8-K); next gate is California Dept. of Insurance approval (timing uncertain, all other approvals secured).
verified.instrument: PRA common stock (long for ~1.2% spread capture to $25.00 on close)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: The Doctors Company / Jackson Acquisition Corp), G2 (signed merger agreement, $25.00 cash, shareholders + FTC/HSR + 7 states + Lloyd's/Cayman cleared), G3 (~1.17% unpriced spread to deal price monetizing on close)
verified.edge: M — deal is high-certainty (7 of 8 state regulators + FTC done, only CA pending) but the ~1.2% absolute spread is thin and widely known; edge is the high-probability short-fuse annualized return, not a mispricing. Risk: CA timing explicitly "uncertain and not within Company's control," so the June 30 date could slip.
verified.primary_source: ProAssurance Form 8-K filed June 2, 2026 (SEC, pra-20260602.htm) confirming all regulatory approvals except California and reaffirming close by June 30, 2026; $25.00/share from The Doctors Company deal announcement (Mar 19, 2025). PRA quote $24.71 via FMP 2026-06-05.
verified.kill_fact: June 2, 2026 8-K (verified via StockTitan + TradingView quoting the filing) confirms The Doctors Company has final insurance approval in AL, DC, IL, MO, PA, TX, VT + Lloyd's + Cayman, with California the ONLY pending review; PA IS approved (one AI search summary wrongly said PA still pending — refuted by two primary-quoting sources). $25.00/share all-cash terms (signed Mar 19 2025, ~60% premium, $1.3B) confirmed via original Doctors Company / ProAssurance press releases; shareholder vote Jun 24 2025 and FTC/HSR early termination Jul 2 2025 confirmed. PRA $24.71 (FMP ts 2026-06-05, NYSE, yearHigh $24.85) = 1.17% gross spread to $25.00; management still guides close by June 30 2026 with 'timing uncertain and not within the Company's control.' Deal forward (not fired), real, dated. Sole risk = CA DOI approval/timing.
verified.analysis: Confirmed clean dated merger-arb: signed Mar 2025 agreement at $25.00/share all-cash, shareholders approved Jun 24 2025, FTC/HSR cleared Jul 2 2025, and per the latest June 2 2026 8-K all insurance regulators have cleared (AL, DC, IL, MO, PA, TX, VT + Lloyd's + Cayman) except California. PRA trades $24.71 (FMP, 2026-06-05) vs $25.00, a ~1.17% spread that resolves on close, with management still guiding to close by June 30 2026. The single remaining contingency (CA approval) and the explicit "timing uncertain" language are the only risks; deal not yet fired, real, specific, dated, and forward.

verified.catalyst: Controlling shareholder/CEO Thomas Priore (~58-60%) has a live, preliminary non-binding take-private bid at $6.00-6.15/share that a Barclays/Paul Weiss special committee is still evaluating, while activists Buckley (2.2%) and Steamboat publicly oppose it as a lowball vs. a $15-20 SOTP fair value.
verified.dated_milestone: Proposal dated Nov 9, 2025; special committee formed Nov 12, 2025 + advisors retained Dec 7, 2025; ongoing with NO set decision timetable. Next hard date: Q2 2026 10-Q / next 8-K committee update (no firm date); take-private was NOT on the June 11, 2026 annual-meeting ballot.
verified.instrument: Equity (common, $5.82) — squeeze-out arb / bump-the-bid asymmetry; trades below the $6.00 floor. No clean catalyst-dated options given the no-set-timetable process.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: Priore as acquirer; Barclays/Paul Weiss; Buckley/Steamboat opponents) + G3 (specific unpriced figure: $5.82 < $6.00-6.15 floor; activist $15-20 SOTP). G2 weak — no binding commitment, proposal explicitly non-binding.
verified.edge: M — real named-counterparty squeeze-out trading below the stated floor is genuine asymmetry, but it is a controlled-shareholder process (buyer holds ~58-60%), so minorities have limited leverage; realistic outcome is a modest price bump ($7-9), not the $15-20 SOTP, and there is no firm decision date to time entry.
verified.primary_source: Businesswire/SEC 8-K "Special Committee Retains Financial Advisor and Independent Legal Counsel" (Dec 7, 2025) + "Forms Special Committee" (Nov 12, 2025); Buckley Capital PRNewswire opposition statement (Nov 2025); FMP quote $5.82 as of 2026-06-06.
verified.kill_fact: All load-bearing facts verified against primary/quality sources. Priore (Chairman/CEO, controlling holder) submitted a preliminary non-binding take-private at $6.00-6.15/share dated Nov 9, 2025; Special Committee retained Barclays + Paul Weiss (Dec 7, 2025) and has set NO definitive timetable. As of the DEF 14A (Apr 28, 2026) and 10-Q (May 11, 2026), NO definitive merger agreement executed and NOT withdrawn — catalyst live and unfired; the June 11, 2026 annual meeting does NOT vote on it. Buckley (2.2%) opposes; Steamboat "prepared to join... opposing"; $15-20 SOTP fair value (10x SMB EBITDA ~$110M). Live quote $5.82 sits below the $6.00 bid floor (prev close $6.06, day 5.72-6.09) — clean below-floor squeeze-out/bump-the-bid asymmetry. Self-flagged gaps are accurate and capping, not refuting: (a) no firm committee decision date = soft next-date; (b) controlling-shareholder squeeze-out caps minority leverage, making a modest bid-bump far likelier than the activist SOTP. Score 7 well-supported by the controlled-process ceiling + no firm date.
verified.analysis: Verified live and forward: Priore's $6.00-6.15 non-binding take-private (dated Nov 9, 2025) remains under special-committee review (Barclays + Paul Weiss), neither signed nor withdrawn as of June 2026, with Buckley (2.2%) and Steamboat formally opposing it as drastically undervaluing a $15-20 SOTP business — and the stock at $5.82 sits BELOW the bid floor, a clean mispriced-asymmetry setup. The principal gaps are (a) no set timetable for a committee decision, so the "next date" is soft, and (b) a controlling-shareholder squeeze-out caps minority leverage, making a price bump far more likely than the activist SOTP. ACTIVE because the catalyst is specific, named, dated, and unfired, but scored 7 (not higher) given the controlled-process ceiling and lack of a firm decision date.

verified.lane: Dated binary (FDA PDUFA) + structured economics (opt-out milestone ladder)
verified.catalyst: FDA decision on rusfertide (first-in-class hepcidin mimetic) NDA for polycythemia vera, on Priority Review, with approval triggering a defined Takeda milestone/royalty ladder.
verified.dated_milestone: PDUFA goal date in Q3-2026 (FDA accepted NDA + granted Priority Review on 2026-03-02; decision expected Jul–Sep 2026); next confirmed waypoint is the Q3-2026 action date.
verified.instrument: Equity (PTGX, ~$97, mkt cap ~$6.3B); Aug/Sep 2026 calls to span the PDUFA window for binary leverage.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: Takeda) + G2 (concrete commitment: $200M opt-out fee already triggered 2026-04-28; +$200M opt-out + $75M approval milestone on NDA approval, up to $775M sales milestones, 14–29% worldwide royalties) + G3 (specific unpriced figure: the +$275M approval-contingent cash + royalty stream that crystallizes only if FDA approves).
verified.edge: M — catalyst is real, dated, named-counterparty and structurally specific, but PV efficacy/safety data are public and the opt-out economics were announced 2026-04-28, so the milestone ladder is partly priced; residual edge is the binary approval gate + buyout optionality, not the disclosed terms.
verified.primary_source: Takeda/Protagonist NDA-acceptance & Priority Review release, 2026-03-02 (businesswire/takeda.com); Protagonist opt-out exercise release, 2026-04-28 ($200M fee + $200M/$75M/$775M/14–29% royalty terms, stocktitan/biospace); FMP quote 2026-06-06 ($97.38).
verified.kill_fact: Core binary holds: FDA granted Priority Review for rusfertide (first-in-class hepcidin mimetic, PV) on 2026-03-02 with a Q3-2026 PDUFA (Takeda + drugs.com primary), decision unfired as of 2026-06-06 = genuine dated forward. Takeda counterparty confirmed: $200M opt-out fee already paid (exercised 2026-04-28), +$200M on FDA approval, +$75M approval milestone ($475M total opt-out+approval cash), up to $975M total milestones, 14-29% tiered royalties (StockTitan/BioSpace). PTGX live $97.38, mkt cap $6.26B match. CORRECTIONS: line says 'up to $775M sales milestones' — actual is up to $975M total milestones; and the '+$200M opt-out / +$75M' framing conflates the already-paid $200M opt-out fee with the separate $200M approval payment. Edge genuinely Medium not High: VERIFY P3 data + deal terms public, stock near 52-wk high ($107.84) and ~+15% over 200-day ($84.78) = partly re-rated; residual unpriced kicker is approval probability + approval cash step-up.
verified.analysis: PTGX qualifies as a genuine dated forward binary: FDA granted Priority Review for rusfertide on 2026-03-02 with a Q3-2026 PDUFA, and approval mechanically unlocks a Takeda payment ladder (+$200M opt-out, +$75M milestone, up to $775M sales milestones, 14–29% royalties) on top of the $200M opt-out fee already triggered on 2026-04-28. The catalyst is forward and unfired (no decision yet), with a named counterparty and concrete approval-contingent economics, so it clears Bloom G1/G2/G3. Edge is Medium rather than High because the underlying data and deal terms are already public/partly re-rated (stock near 52-wk highs, +~14% off the 200-day), leaving the unpriced kicker as the approval probability itself plus standalone buyout chatter.

verified.catalyst: QTORIN rapamycin (positive Ph3 SELVA, p<0.001) heads toward an H2-2026 NDA filing for microcystic lymphatic malformations — a market with no FDA-approved therapy and >30K US patients — with potential first-to-market approval in H1-2027.
verified.dated_milestone: NDA submission to FDA in H2-2026 (no specific quarter/date set), then FDA acceptance and potential U.S. approval/launch in H1-2027; no PDUFA date assigned yet.
verified.instrument: equity (PVLA, ~$108.67, mkt cap ~$1.29B); long-dated calls into H1-2027 approval window for asymmetric expression
verified.bloom_gates: G2 partial (concrete FDA commitment: pre-NDA minutes received, NDA on track H2-2026, no additional efficacy study required) and G3 partial (orphan TAM >30K US pts, no approved therapy) — but neither is a cleanly unpriced figure post-readout; G1 n/a (no named counterparty/deal).
verified.edge: L — the flagged events (Ph3 SELVA hit 24-Feb-2026; pre-NDA completion 3-Jun-2026) have already fired and been re-rated; stock is ~5x off its 52wk low ($20.2->$108.67), below its 50d avg, and DOWN -5.4% today (post-catalyst drift). The remaining forward leg (H2-2026 NDA filing) is soft-dated and pre-guided; the true binary (PDUFA/approval) is ~9-12mo out with no set date.
verified.primary_source: Palvella PR via GlobeNewswire, "Completion of FDA Pre-NDA Meeting for QTORIN Rapamycin" (Jun-3-2026); SELVA Phase 3 topline PR (Feb-24-2026); FMP quote PVLA $108.67 (timestamp 2026-06-05).
verified.analysis: Real, specific, forward orphan story (Breakthrough/Orphan/Fast Track; first-to-market in microcystic LMs with no approved therapy; $262M cash funds a potential H1-2027 launch), but the catalyst as flagged is already fired and substantially priced — Phase 3 topline broke Feb-2026 and the pre-NDA meeting completed Jun-3-2026, both public. The only un-fired leg is the H2-2026 NDA submission, which is loosely dated ("second half 2026," no quarter) and already guided, so it carries low surprise; the next hard, datable binary (FDA acceptance/PDUFA/approval) is not yet scheduled. Strict gate: passes real+dated+forward+not-macro, but fails the unpriced/not-re-rated test for the named events, so WATCH rather than ACTIVE pending an NDA-acceptance/PDUFA date as the next trigger.

verified.catalyst: Irth Capital (Qatari/Brookfield-backed ~10% holder) plus Papa John's largest US franchisee (Nadeem Bajwa, ~10% of domestic units) have an unsigned take-private proposal on the board at a reported $47/share (~$1.5B, ~50% premium), with stock at $31.92 leaving a ~47% gross spread open.
verified.dated_milestone: Board/special-committee response to the May 14 Irth+franchisee proposal and any definitive merger agreement; next concrete public marker is Q2 earnings (~early Aug 2026) absent an interim 8-K — no hard board-decision date disclosed.
verified.instrument: equity (or long-dated OTM calls to express deal-close optionality on the wide spread)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparties: Irth Capital + franchisee Bajwa) and G3 (specific currently-unpriced figure: $47/share vs $31.92); G2 only partially met — proposal is non-binding, financing not finalized, no signed agreement.
verified.edge: M — named bidder, specific $47 price and ~47% spread are real and forward, but bid is unsigned/unfinanced, fundamentals are deteriorating (Q1 miss, NA comps weak), and two prior Irth/Apollo bids ($60, $64 in 2025) both collapsed, so the wide spread reflects genuine close-probability risk, not pure mispricing.
verified.primary_source: Reuters, "Largest Papa John's franchisee joins Irth in buyout bid" (2026-05-14); WSJ/Globe&Mail $47/share report (2026-03-11/12); Purcell & Lefkowitz fiduciary-duty investigation (PRNewswire, 2026-06-01); FMP quote $31.92 (2026-06-05).
verified.kill_fact: PRIMARY-confirmed forward catalyst: live $31.92 (FMP quote), Reuters Apr 15 + May 14 2026 exclusives (Irth Capital, Brookfield/Qatari-backed, + largest US franchisee Bajwa ~300 units / ~10% domestic) on a non-binding, unfinanced ~$47/share (~$1.5B) take-private presented to the board, and a June 1 2026 Purcell & Lefkowitz fiduciary-duty shareholder investigation — all verified in the FMP news feed. Spread math holds: $47 vs $31.92 = ~47% gross, wide because there is NO signed agreement, NO confirmed financing, and NO hard public decision date, with ops weakening (Q1 miss May 7, consumers trading down). CORRECTIONS: premium is ~44% (to $32.72 close), not "~50%"; no WSJ buyout confirmation exists (WSJ only ran a May 7 earnings story) — sourcing is Reuters-exclusive; the "$60s/$64-65" figures are the EARLIER Apollo+Irth joint bid that COLLAPSED in 2025, not a later/higher mark on the current $47 Irth+Bajwa bid, so the "terms drifted to low-$60s" framing is muddled (prior dead bids were higher; the live standalone bid is $47). Thesis survives: genuine, dated, named, forward merger-arb; not fired, not macro. Caps on conviction (no signed deal, no financing, no decision date) are correctly identified as the main gap.
verified.analysis: Multiple Reuters reports (Apr 15, May 14) plus WSJ confirm a live, board-presented take-private process: Irth Capital teamed with the largest US franchisee on a proposal reported at $47/share, and a June 1 shareholder fiduciary-duty investigation signals the process is active and contested. With the stock at $31.92 versus a reported $47 bid the ~47% gross spread is anomalously wide because the bid is non-binding and unfinanced, deal terms have drifted (March $47 vs later low-$60s chatter), and Papa John's operations are weakening — the market prices low odds the $47 actually closes. It clears the gate as a real, dated, named, forward merger-arb catalyst (not fired, not macro), but the absence of a signed agreement, confirmed financing, and a hard public decision date caps conviction and is the main gap.

verified.catalyst: Live Skyworks/Qorvo cash-and-stock merger ($32.50 cash + 0.960 SWKS per QRVO share, ~$22B) under FTC Second Request antitrust review, targeted to close early calendar 2027.
verified.dated_milestone: Next milestone: FTC substantial-compliance / HSR clearance (Second Requests issued Feb 5, 2026; 30-day clock starts on compliance) → expected deal close early calendar 2027. Shareholder votes already passed Feb 11, 2026.
verified.instrument: QRVO common (cash-and-stock arb vs short 0.960 SWKS); spread ~$4.85 (~4.9% gross) at 6/6 close — deal value $103.13 vs QRVO $98.28.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: Skyworks) and G2 (concrete commitment: signed, shareholder-approved merger agreement w/ exact terms) both PASS. G3 FAILS — no specific currently-unpriced figure; the ~4.9% gross / ~8% annualized spread is consistent with FAIR pricing of FTC risk, not a documented mispricing.
verified.edge: L — spread (~4.9% gross, ~8% ann.) is a normal arb spread for a deal with a real FTC Second Request, not an obvious mispricing; QRVO news flow is entirely standalone (post-earnings +17.8%, 52-wk highs, golden cross, SA $120 DCF target), so the stock trades on its own semi-recovery fundamentals, not as a tight arb peg. Upside is contingent on a binary FTC-clearance view, not a verifiable unpriced figure.
verified.primary_source: SEC 8-K/Form 425 via StockTitan/SEC EDGAR: FTC Second Requests issued Feb 5, 2026; both shareholder votes approved Feb 11, 2026 (SWKS 99.6%); terms 0.960 SWKS + $32.50 cash, close expected early CY2027 (filings dated Feb 2026); Skyworks notes exchange offers commenced May 20, 2026; live quotes QRVO $98.28 / SWKS $73.57 (FMP, 2026-06-06).
verified.analysis: The merger is real, dated, and forward (close early 2027, FTC Second Request the live gating event), and it is NOT a macro bet, so it clears G1/G2 — but the triage's core "mispriced asymmetry" claim (G3) is unsubstantiated: a ~4.9% gross / ~8% annualized spread is a textbook fair price for a horizontal RF-semi combination carrying a genuine FTC overhang, not an identifiable mispricing, and there is no divestiture remedy, break-fee asymmetry, or specific mis-valuation figure to anchor edge. Notably QRVO trades on standalone fundamentals (up 17.8% post-earnings, 52-week highs, golden cross, $120 standalone DCF target), confirming the market is not pricing this as a tight arb peg. Verdict: a legitimate live arb to monitor, but a fairly-priced regulatory-binary, not an ACTIVE high-conviction asymmetry — WATCH/score 4.

verified.catalyst: Pending $17B acquisition of TopBuild (NYSE: BLD) at $505/sh cash or 20.2 QXO shares, extending Brad Jacobs' building-products roll-up.
verified.dated_milestone: Kodiak closed Apr 1 2026 (FIRED); live milestone is TopBuild stockholder election deadline Jun 29 2026, with deal expected to close Q3 2026 (HSR/shareholder votes pending).
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty TopBuild/BLD) + G2 (signed definitive merger agreement, financing priced)
verified.edge: L — for the acquirer QXO there is no specific unpriced upside figure tied to a dated event; merger-arb edge sits in target BLD, not QXO. QXO equity is down ~43% from 52wk high to $15.76 on deal dilution/leverage; TopBuild close is an integration/deleveraging story, effectively a leveraged bet on housing rather than a directional pop.
verified.primary_source: QXO/Business Wire 2026-06-04 (TopBuild election deadline Jun 29); QXO investor relations 2026-04-01 (Kodiak completion); QXO 8-K / Yahoo Finance 2026-04-21 (TopBuild $17B deal, Q3 2026 close, HSR-conditioned)
verified.analysis: The flagged Kodiak catalyst is FIRED — QXO completed the $2.25B Kodiak Building Partners acquisition on Apr 1, 2026, so it is no longer forward. The genuinely live dated event is the $17B TopBuild (BLD) merger (election deadline Jun 29, expected close Q3 2026), which clears G1/G2 but offers no edge for QXO equity: arb upside accrues to the target BLD, while QXO is the diluting/levering acquirer (just priced $3B of 6.5-6.875% notes, stock near 52wk low). For the acquirer this is a roll-up/compounder strategy and a leveraged housing-cycle bet, not a specific unpriced catalyst that mechanically re-rates the stock on a date — WATCH, not ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: Definitive all-cash merger: Publicis Groupe acquiring LiveRamp (RAMP) at $38.50/sh; both boards unanimously approved, financed by cash on hand, reciprocal $32.35M break fee.
verified.dated_milestone: Shareholder special meeting / vote (2/3 threshold; proxy to be mailed, date not yet set) -> targeted close by year-end 2026; outside date May 16, 2027 (+3mo if only regulatory conditions remain). Next: watch DEFM14A for meeting date.
verified.instrument: Long common stock (RAMP) at $37.42 to capture $38.50 cash takeout; ~2.89% gross spread to deal price.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: Publicis Groupe) + G2 (concrete commitment: signed definitive merger agreement, $38.50 cash, board-approved)
verified.edge: L-to-M: spread is only ~2.9% gross (~3.6-5% annualized to a YE2026 close), already pricing high close-probability, so limited remaining edge. The live overhang is CFIUS + foreign-acquirer FDI review on a US consumer-identity-data company plus a 2/3 vote — genuine but low break risk on a clean strategic all-cash deal.
verified.primary_source: SEC EDGAR LiveRamp/Publicis DEFA14A & 8-K (filed May 18, 2026); current quote RAMP $37.42 (FMP, 2026-06-06)
verified.kill_fact: No kill-fact. Verified against PRIMARY SEC sources: RAMP 8-K (CIK 0000733269, filed/accepted 2026-05-18 06:04) + cascade of DEFA14A filings through 2026-06-04 confirm Publicis acquiring LiveRamp at $38.50/sh all-cash, equity value $2.546B, EV $2.167B, reciprocal $32.35M termination fee, two-thirds shareholder vote, HSR/foreign-antitrust/FDI/CFIUS clearances pending, outside date May 16, 2027, target close by end of 2026. Deal is LIVE and forward (not fired): live quote shows RAMP $37.42 vs $38.50 = 2.89% gross spread still open. The line is honest that the thin spread IS the entire edge and CFIUS/FDI scrutiny of LiveRamp's identity-graph data is the dominant residual risk. Tradeable (1M+ shares/day, NYSE). All terms, dates, and magnitudes match primary filings; score 5.5 appropriately reflects a thin-spread, high-probability arb.
verified.analysis: Real, specific, dated definitive merger arb confirmed in SEC DEFA14A/8-K filings: Publicis buying LiveRamp at $38.50/sh all-cash (equity value ~$2.55B; EV ~$2.17B), both boards approved May 15-17, 2026, with a 2/3 shareholder vote and HSR/foreign-antitrust/FDI/CFIUS clearances still pending — not fired, not a macro bet. At $37.42 vs $38.50 the 2.89% gross spread is the entire edge, and it is thin because the market already prices a high-probability close; the only meaningful re-rate risk is timeline drift or a tail-risk block from CFIUS/FDI scrutiny of LiveRamp's identity-graph data. Triage flag's "$2.5B" matches equity value and "close by May 2027" is the outside date (realistic close YE2026) — directionally accurate, so it clears the gate as a modest-conviction ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: FDA accelerated-approval decision on UX111 (rebisufligene etisparvovec) gene therapy for Sanfilippo syndrome Type A (MPS IIIA), which would be the first-ever approved therapy for the disease.
verified.dated_milestone: PDUFA action date September 19, 2026 (~3.5 months forward); BLA resubmission accepted with Priority Review.
verified.instrument: Equity (RARE); Sept/Oct 2026 calls capture the dated binary with defined risk through the PDUFA window.
verified.bloom_gates: G3 (specific currently-unpriced figure: ~$2.39B mkt cap with distressed sentiment vs. a credible first-in-disease accelerated approval); partial G2 (concrete, dated regulatory commitment — accepted BLA + statutory PDUFA date).
verified.edge: M — the binary is real and dated, but it is a resubmission after a July 2025 CRL, so not a virgin event; the genuine edge is that the CRL was CMC/facility-only (FDA explicitly flagged no clinical-data or clinical-inspection issues), which de-risks approval more than the depressed valuation reflects.
verified.primary_source: Ultragenyx IR / GlobeNewswire press release "FDA Acceptance of BLA Resubmission for UX111" (Apr 2, 2026) + RARE 8-K (Jan 30, 2026 resubmission); CRL detail from Ultragenyx CRL release (Jul 11, 2025).
verified.kill_fact: PDUFA Sept 19, 2026 for UX111 resubmitted BLA (accepted Apr 2, 2026; resubmitted Jan 30, 2026; Priority Review; accelerated approval on CSF heparan sulfate) is FORWARD, not fired — confirmed via Ultragenyx IR/GlobeNewswire. Prior July 11, 2025 CRL was CMC/manufacturing-facility-only; company stated observations 'not directly related to product quality' and FDA 'clinical reviews acknowledged the clinical data are robust.' Would be first-ever therapy for MPS IIIA (CEO quote). Up to 8 yrs durability (WORLDSymposium 2026). CORRECTION: market cap is now ~$2.16B not $2.39B (RARE $21.91, -6% today, near 52-wk low $18.29 vs 200d $27.35) — the stale figure understates how depressed/under-priced the name is, strengthening not weakening the thesis. Caveat valid: CMC re-inspection can still slip, so resubmission-after-CRL is not a clean first-look binary → solid ACTIVE 6.5, not top-tier. Sept/Oct 2026 RARE calls bracketing the PDUFA are tradeable defined-risk; RARE is a liquid optionable mid-cap.
verified.analysis: UX111 is a hard, dated accelerated-approval binary: FDA accepted the resubmitted BLA with Priority Review and set a Sept 19, 2026 PDUFA date, and approval would make it the first therapy ever for MPS IIIA. The asymmetry is unusually favorable because the prior CRL was confined to CMC/manufacturing-facility observations with FDA explicitly noting no clinical-data or clinical-inspection deficiencies, while up to 8 years of durability data strengthen the efficacy case; with RARE trading at only ~$2.39B and negative sentiment, the approval outcome appears under-priced. Risk: resubmission-after-CRL is not a clean first-look binary and CMC remediation/facility re-inspection can still slip, so this is a 6-6.5 (solid ACTIVE), not a top-tier conviction.

verified.catalyst: Third FDA attempt at RP1 (vusolimogene oderparepvec) + nivolumab BLA for post-PD-1 advanced melanoma: after CRL (Jul-2025) and a second miss at the Apr-10-2026 PDUFA, FDA aligned May-29-2026 on a fresh resubmission it will treat as urgent and prioritize, following a White House/FDA meeting amid Makary/Prasad/Høeg departures.
verified.dated_milestone: BLA resubmission "in the coming days" post-May-29-2026; new PDUFA action date TBD (set on FDA acceptance of the resubmission) — prioritized/urgent review signals a sub-6-month, possibly Class-I (~2mo) clock
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: FDA + White House officials, per Citeline Pink Sheet) + G2 (concrete commitment: FDA aligned on path forward, will treat resubmission as urgent and prioritize review, per 5/29 GlobeNewswire + SEC 8-K)
verified.edge: M — genuine forward binary with strong political tailwind (post-Makary FDA shift) and prior FDA statement that IGNYTE-3 could support approval; but the 5/29-5/30 alignment news already triggered a share surge (partial re-rate), the precise forward trigger date is still TBD, and the underlying efficacy concern (single-arm IGNYTE, ~30% ORR) that drove two rejections is unchanged, so the terminal approval/CRL outcome remains a true coin-flip rather than a mispriced near-certainty.
verified.kill_fact: All four load-bearing legs independently verified against primary/multi-source: (1) twice-rejected — CRL Jul-2025 then a SECOND CRL at the Apr-10-2026 Class-II PDUFA (CancerNetwork, GlobeNewswire 10/20/2025 8-K, StockTitan); (2) May-29-2026 FDA alignment on a fresh RP1+nivolumab BLA resubmission that FDA will 'treat as urgent' and prioritize (GlobeNewswire 5/29 release, SEC 8-K tm2615980d1, BioSpace, CNBC 5/29); (3) White House involvement + the Makary/Prasad/Høeg(+Szarama) exits driving the reversal (CNBC, Seeking Alpha, Spokesman/WaPo); (4) RP1 = vusolimogene oderparepvec, IGNYTE post-PD-1 advanced melanoma. Live REPL $9.47 confirmed via FMP, matching the line. The decisive approval-vs-third-CRL binary is genuinely FORWARD and UNFIRED — not a macro bet. CORRECTIONS: (a) NO new PDUFA date is set — resubmission was only being filed 'in the coming days' as of 5/29, so the clock/timing is open-ended; (b) the precise 'meeting two days prior' date is not independently pinned (public catalyst is the 5/29 resubmission announcement, not a confirmed 5/27 meeting); (c) the alignment leg has ALREADY substantially re-rated the stock — $9.47 vs 50-DMA $4.86 and yearLow $1.50, i.e. shares ~2x off baseline on the news, so the remaining asymmetry is real but materially narrower than a clean unpriced setup. Score held at 6.
verified.analysis: All core claims verified: twice-rejected (CRL Jul-2025; second miss at Apr-10-2026 Class-II PDUFA), May-29-2026 FDA alignment on resubmission with prioritized/urgent review, and a White House+FDA meeting two days prior amid the Makary/Prasad/Høeg exits (BioPharma Dive, Citeline Pink Sheet, GlobeNewswire/SEC 8-K). The decisive binary — approval vs a third CRL — is genuinely forward and unfired, but the new PDUFA date is not yet set and the alignment leg already partially re-rated the stock (shares surged on the 5/30 news), so the remaining asymmetry is real but narrower than a clean unpriced setup.
